serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Cold Shredded Potato Salad is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a small side-dish bowl as commonly served in Chinese home cooking or street food stalls.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Potatoes, boiled, cooked without skin, flesh, without salt (FDC ID 170026) adapted with dressing components (sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Chinese cold shredded potato salad exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB. Values are adapted from USDA boiled potato data (FDC ID 170026) with adjustments for added dressing ingredients (sesame oil ~3.8 g fat per 100 g finished dish, soy sauce ~280 mg sodium per 100 g finished dish, rice vinegar). Blanching and chilling method retains more starch structure than boiling, consistent with the crisp texture described. Input AI-generated values cross-checked against USDA component data and retained where consistent.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of potatoes: effect of cooking method and cooling — analogous estimate based on boiled then chilled potato with vinegar dressing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / peer-reviewed literature (Fernandes et al., 2005, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chinese cold shredded potato salad (Liangban Tudousi). GI estimated at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for boiled then chilled potato (GI ~56) and peer-reviewed evidence that vinegar/acid dressings further reduce glycemic response by approximately 20–35% (Liljeberg & Bjorck, 1998, European Journal of Clinical Nutrition). Blanching followed by cold rinsing increases resistant starch content, consistent with a low-to-medium GI. GL per 150 g serving = (55 × 12.2 × 1.5) / 100 = 10.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce dressing (280 mg per 100 g); partially offset by high potassium content (380 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or reduce soy sauce quantity to keep sodium intake within daily targets; the high potassium content (380 mg/100 g) provides a beneficial counterbalance to sodium and supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) due to blanching, chilling, and vinegar dressing; moderate carbohydrate content (12.2 g/100 g) with 1.8 g dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cold Shredded Potato Salad has a low estimated GI (~55) attributable to resistant starch formation during chilling and the acetic acid in the vinegar dressing, making it a more suitable potato preparation for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes compared to hot boiled or mashed potato; portion control to one 150 g serving is recommended to limit glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.5 g/100 g), predominantly unsaturated fat from sesame oil, and significant potassium (380 mg/100 g) supporting vascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat and provides potassium and vitamin C, which support cardiovascular health; however, sodium from soy sauce (280 mg/100 g) should be monitored and soy sauce quantity reduced for individuals on sodium-restricted cardiac diets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potato is a nightshade vegetable; some individuals with rheumatoid arthritis report sensitivity to nightshade alkaloids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Most individuals with rheumatoid arthritis can consume this dish without issue; those with known nightshade sensitivity should monitor individual response and consider substituting with a non-nightshade vegetable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cold Shredded Potato Salad, known in Mandarin as Liángbàn Tǔdòusī (凉拌土豆丝), is a quintessential Northern Chinese and Sichuan-influenced cold side dish. The dish is prepared by julienning raw potatoes into thin shreds, rinsing them thoroughly in cold water to remove surface starch, briefly blanching them in boiling water for 30–60 seconds to achieve a crisp, al dente texture, and then immediately plunging them into ice water to halt cooking. The chilled potato shreds are dressed with a light, tangy mixture of rice vinegar, light soy sauce, minced garlic, sesame oil, and dried chili flakes. Sichuan peppercorns are often briefly infused in hot oil before being poured over the dish, imparting a characteristic numbing (má) sensation. The result is a refreshing, crunchy, mildly spicy, and tangy salad that contrasts sharply with the creamy, soft texture of Western-style potato salads. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 85 kcal, 1.5 g protein, 3.8 g fat (predominantly unsaturated from sesame oil), and 12.2 g carbohydrate with 1.8 g dietary fibre. Sodium is moderate at 280 mg/100 g due to soy sauce; potassium is notably high at 380 mg/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 55 (low), attributable to resistant starch formation during chilling and the acetic acid in the vinegar dressing. The dish is vegan, dairy-free, and suitable for most dietary patterns. It is a staple in Chinese home cooking and street food culture, commonly served as a side dish or appetizer at lunch and dinner.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Chinese cold shredded potato salad exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB, or other approved primary sources. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA boiled potato data with dressing component adjustments. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for chilled boiled potato and peer-reviewed vinegar-effect literature. Input AI-generated values were cross-checked against USDA component data and retained where consistent. Medium confidence is appropriate given the adaptation basis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central boiled potato (FDC ID 170026) with dressing component adjustments for sesame oil, soy sauce, and rice vinegar; no exact approved-source entry exists for this preparation. 2. Saturated fat (0.5 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.4 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.6 g) estimated from sesame oil fatty acid profile per USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 04058) at approximately 3.8 g total fat per 100 g finished dish. 3. Trans fat is 0.0 g: confirmed negligible for this plant-based preparation with no hydrogenated fats. 4. Cholesterol is 0.0 g: naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients. 5. Vitamin A is 0.0 mcg RAE: naturally zero as potatoes and the dressing ingredients contain negligible beta-carotene or retinol. 6. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 mcg: naturally zero as this is a vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients. 7. Vitamin D is 0.0 mcg: naturally zero as potatoes and plant-based dressing ingredients contain no vitamin D. 8. water_g set to -1: moisture data for this specific cold-dressed preparation not available in USDA FoodData Central or other approved sources as of 2026-04-17. 9. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database chilled boiled potato data and peer-reviewed vinegar-effect literature; this is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement. 10. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as (55 × 12.2 × 1.5) / 100 = 10.1. 11. Input record contained duplicate portion entries (both 150 g); deduplicated to single default serving of 150 g. 12. FoodOn match FOODON_03311737 (potato salad) is approximate; no specific class for Chinese cold julienned potato salad exists. FoodEx2 code A00GJ (potato dishes) is approximate.
